title: Anneslia cumingii
intro: Anneslia cumingii, also known as Philippine mullein or Philippine velvet plant, is a medicinal plant species native to the Philippines. This plant has been traditionally used for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are thought to aid in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Anneslia cumingii include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Anneslia cumingii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Anneslia cumingii is classified under the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This family consists of around 196 genera and over 2,000 species, with Anneslia cumingii being one of the lesser-known species. The Annonaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world, particularly in Central and South America. Anneslia cumingii is further categorized under the genus Anneslia, which is a small genus of flowering plants. The exact classification of Anneslia cumingii may vary depending on the source, but it is generally classified within the Annonaceae family.
